column3: Hot **lava** flowed slowly down the mountainside.
Deep underground the **magma** stays extremely hot.
The ancient **volcano** erupted last summer.
A strong **earthquake** shook the entire city.
The meteor left a huge **crater** behind.
A steaming **geyser** sprayed water into the sky.
Scientists found a dinosaur **fossil** in the cliff.
Thick **sediment** settled at the bottom of the lake.
Soil **erosion** happens quickly on bare hillsides.
Bronze is an **alloy** of copper and tin.
Harmful **bacteria** can grow in warm water.
An **enzyme** helps break down food in the stomach.
Each **gene** carries specific biological information.
Human **dna** contains billions of tiny instructions.
Each **chromosome** holds many important genes.
The cell **membrane** controls what enters the cell.
Muscle **tissue** is made of long thin fibers.
A strange **fungus** was growing on the log.
Green **algae** covered the surface of the pond.
Tiny **plankton** float near the ocean surface.
A young **larva** hatches from the tiny egg.
This tiny **microbe** can only be seen under glass.
Green **mold** was growing on the old bread.
A single **spore** can grow into a new fungus.
A whale is a large ocean **mammal**.
A snake is a cold-blooded **reptile**.
A frog is a common **amphibian** near ponds.
The beetle is a hard-shelled **insect**.
A dog is a four-legged **vertebrate**.
A worm is a soft **invertebrate** without bones.
A rabbit is a small gentle **herbivore**.
The tiger is a powerful **carnivore**.
A bear is an **omnivore** that eats almost anything.
A tick is a blood-sucking **parasite**.
Green plants make food through **photosynthesis**.
The **chlorophyll** gives leaves their green color.
Cellular **respiration** releases energy from food.
Yeast causes the **fermentation** of sugar into alcohol.
The **mitochondria** produce energy inside every cell.
She placed a plant **specimen** on the glass slide.
A desert is a very dry **biome**.
This jungle has amazing **biodiversity**.
Each animal **phylum** shares certain basic features.
**genus** is a group of closely related species.
Long fur is a useful **adaptation** for cold climates.
A random **mutation** changed the color of the fly.
A glass **pipette** sits on the lab bench.
A tall glass **burette** drips slowly into the beaker.
The **centrifuge** spins tubes at very high speed.
The chemist added a new **reagent** to the tube.
The color **indicator** showed the acid was strong.
Blue **litmus** turns red in an acidic solution.
She performed a careful **titration** in the lab.
Full **saturation** means no more salt will dissolve.
A white **precipitate** formed at the bottom.
**distillation** produces very pure water.
**chromatography** separates different colors in ink.
The **oxidation** of iron causes rusty spots.
**combustion** needs fuel and oxygen to happen.
Plastic is a very common artificial **polymer**.
A **catalyst** speeds up a chemical reaction.
A salt crystal breaks into a positive **ion**.
**carbon** forms the base of all living things.
**hydrogen** is the lightest known element.
Plants need **nitrogen** to grow healthy leaves.
Hot **plasma** fills the core of every star.
This radioactive **isotope** decays very slowly.
Harmful **radiation** comes from some heavy metals.
A **radioactive** isotope glows inside the sealed container.
Stars produce energy through nuclear **fusion**.
Uranium can split through nuclear **fission**.
High **velocity** can be dangerous for drivers.
The rocket showed amazing **acceleration** during launch.
A heavy truck has huge **momentum** on the road.
**friction** between the wheels and road slows the car.
The deep ocean has very high **pressure**.
A long **lever** helps lift the heavy rock.
She used a **pulley** to raise the bucket.
A simple **wedge** can split thick wood easily.
**inertia** keeps objects moving in a straight line.
Lead has a very high **density**.
**buoyancy** keeps heavy ships afloat on water.
The clock **pendulum** swings back and forth.
A strong **vibration** shook the small table.
A large **amplitude** means a very loud sound.
A glass **prism** splits sunlight into many colors.
An **electromagnetic** signal travels at light speed.
Stable **equilibrium** means the forces balance each other.
**thermodynamics** studies heat and energy flow.
A curved **trajectory** carried the rocket into space.
**sublimation** turns dry ice directly into gas.
A small **meteorite** landed in the desert.
The huge colorful **nebula** shines through the telescope.
**astronomy** studies stars and distant galaxies.
**geology** explains how rocks and mountains form.
**ecology** studies how living things share the earth.
**magnetism** pulls iron objects toward the bar.
Copper wire is an excellent **conductor** of electricity.
Rubber is a good electrical **insulator**.
A **seismograph** measures the strength of earthquakes.
